title: How The AdvanSix (ASIX) Investment Story Is Shifting With New Assumptions And Risks
content:
Never miss an important update on your stock portfolio and cut through the noise. Over 7 million investors trust Simply Wall St to stay informed where it matters for FREE.

The latest update for AdvanSix lifts the fair value estimate from US$22.50 to US$23.50, a modest reset that frames where some analysts currently see the share price potential. That change sits alongside research describing a mixed setup, with cost and capital discipline on one side, and input cost and EBITDA pressures on the other. Read on to see how these moving parts fit together and how you can keep track of the evolving AdvanSix story.

Stay updated as the Fair Value for AdvanSix shifts by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on AdvanSix.

What Wall Street Has Been Saying

🐂 Bullish Takeaways

Truist recently raised its AdvanSix price target to US$20 from US$19, signaling that its valuation work supports some upside from prior expectations, while it keeps a Hold rating. Truist highlights the Plant Nutrients business, noting what it views as a relatively favorable growth outlook for that segment, which it sees as an important contributor to the overall equity story. Efforts to rationalize costs and capital spending are flagged by Truist as a positive. The firm expects these actions to support positive free cash flow generation in 2026.

🐻 Bearish Takeaways

Truist points to input cost inflation as a near term headwind that is currently outweighing the benefits it sees in the Plant Nutrients growth outlook. The firm also anticipates meaningful year over year EBITDA pressure, which it cites as a key reason for maintaining a Hold stance rather than a more positive rating.

Do your thoughts align with the Bull or Bear Analysts? Perhaps you think there's more to the story. Head to the Simply Wall St Community to discover more perspectives!NYSE:ASIX 1-Year Stock Price Chart

We've flagged 1 risk for AdvanSix. See which could impact your investment.

How This Changes the Fair Value For AdvanSix

Fair value moves from US$22.50 to US$23.50 within the updated framework. Revenue growth assumption is 3.37%, unchanged from the prior model. Net profit margin assumption remains at 4.20%. Future P/E moves from 11.0x to 11.4x in the updated model. Discount rate is set at 8.36%, compared with the prior 8.38%.

Never Miss an Update: Follow The Narrative

Narratives connect a company's business story to the assumptions behind its earnings forecasts and fair value estimates. They refresh as new data, estimates, and risks emerge so you can see how the thesis is evolving in one place.

Story Continues

Head over to the Simply Wall St Community and follow the Narrative on AdvanSix to stay up to date on:

How ammonium sulfate capacity expansion, the SUSTAIN program, and a strong order book shape the outlook for the Plant Nutrients and broader chemical operations. The role of 45Q carbon capture tax credits and insurance settlements in supporting net income, cash flow, and earnings stability. Key operational and market risks, including nylon oversupply, rising raw material costs, pressure from Chinese exports in ag chemicals, and higher growth capex.

title: How The AdvanSix (ASIX) Narrative Is Evolving With Cost Discipline And 2026 Cash Flow Hopes
content:
Track your investments for FREE with Simply Wall St, the portfolio command center trusted by over 7 million individual investors worldwide.

AdvanSix is back in focus as analysts update their narrative around a price target in the higher US$20 range for the shares. That target is tied to views on the company’s cost and capital spending efforts, a steadier outlook for the Plant Nutrients business, and questions about how input cost inflation could affect earnings and sentiment. As you read on, you will see how these moving pieces shape the evolving story and what to watch as the thesis develops.

Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the full story. Head over to our Company Report to find new ways to value AdvanSix.

What Wall Street Has Been Saying

🐂 Bullish Takeaways

Truist lifted its AdvanSix price target to US$20 from US$19, which signals some support for the shares even while the rating remains Hold. Truist highlights AdvanSix's efforts to rationalize costs and capital spending, and sees these actions as a path to positive free cash flow in 2026. The firm also points to a relatively favorable outlook for the Plant Nutrients business, which it views as an important earnings contributor once cost pressures ease.

🐻 Bearish Takeaways

Truist cautions that input cost inflation is currently weighing on the investment case, limiting the impact of the Plant Nutrients growth opportunity. The firm expects meaningful year over year EBITDA pressure, which underpins its Hold stance and suggests that execution on cost and capital discipline will be closely watched.

Do your thoughts align with the Bull or Bear Analysts? Perhaps you think there's more to the story. Head to the Simply Wall St Community to discover more perspectives!NYSE:ASIX 1-Year Stock Price Chart

We've flagged 1 risk for AdvanSix. See which could impact your investment.

What's in the News

AdvanSix reported that between October 1, 2025 and January 30, 2026 it did not repurchase any shares under its existing buyback program, with 0 shares bought for US$0 million in this period. The company has completed the repurchase of 507,462 shares under the buyback announced on February 17, 2023, representing 1.89% of shares for a total of US$13.28 million. This update on the buyback program outlines how much capital has been directed to share repurchases over the life of this authorization compared with other potential uses.

How This Changes the Fair Value For AdvanSix

Fair value remains modeled at US$22.5 per share, with no change to the central valuation anchor. Long-term revenue growth is effectively unchanged at about 3.37% in both the previous and updated model. Assumed net profit margin stays close to 4.20% under the updated inputs. The future P/E assumption edges down from 11.02x to 10.96x in the revised model. The discount rate moves from 8.57% to 8.38%, modestly lowering the implied cost of capital used.

Story Continues

The role of 45Q carbon capture tax credits and insurance settlements in shaping net income, cash flow and margin assumptions over time. Key pressure points such as oversupply in global nylon markets, higher raw material costs, Chinese competition in ag chemicals and rising growth capex.

title: How The AdvanSix (ASIX) Investment Story Is Shifting With New Valuation And Cash Flow Expectations
content:
Find winning stocks in any market cycle. Join 7 million investors using Simply Wall St's investing ideas for FREE.

AdvanSix is back on investors' radar after a fresh update that lifts its fair value estimate from US$21.50 to US$22.50, alongside a recent US$20 price target from parts of the Street. Supporters point to this adjustment as evidence that ongoing cost work, capital discipline, and the outlook for the Plant Nutrients business still leave room for upside, while skeptics see input cost pressure and EBITDA risk keeping the risk and reward trade off fairly balanced. As you read on, you will see how the evolving analyst narrative might shape your own view of the stock.

Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the full story. Head over to our Company Report to find new ways to value AdvanSix.

What Wall Street Has Been Saying

🐂 Bullish Takeaways

Truist lifted its AdvanSix price target to US$20 from US$19 while maintaining a Hold rating, suggesting the firm sees some incremental upside potential at current levels. Truist highlights AdvanSix's efforts to rationalize costs and capital spending, which the firm believes can support positive free cash flow generation in 2026 even while EBITDA faces pressure. The Truist note points to a relatively favorable growth outlook for the Plant Nutrients business as a key support for the longer term earnings profile.

🐻 Bearish Takeaways

Truist flags input cost inflation as a near term headwind that is currently more impactful than the positive elements of the Plant Nutrients outlook. The firm also calls out meaningful year over year EBITDA pressure, which, in its view, keeps risk and reward more balanced despite the higher price target.

Do your thoughts align with the Bull or Bear Analysts? Perhaps you think there's more to the story. Head to the Simply Wall St Community to discover more perspectives!NYSE:ASIX 1-Year Stock Price Chart

We've flagged 1 risk for AdvanSix. See which could impact your investment.

How This Changes the Fair Value For AdvanSix

The fair value estimate has been adjusted from US$21.50 to US$22.50. The assumed long term revenue growth rate has been updated from about 2.41% to about 3.37%. The assumed net profit margin has moved from about 4.31% to about 4.20%. The future P/E multiple has been updated from about 10.64x to about 11.02x. The discount rate has been revised from 8.95% to about 8.57%.

How ammonium sulfate capacity expansion, a strong order book, and demand for sulfur nutrition in crops are feeding into the Plant Nutrients growth story. The impact of 45Q carbon capture tax credits, insurance settlements, and operational excellence programs like SUSTAIN on earnings stability and cash flow. Key pressure points such as raw material cost headwinds, global nylon oversupply, Chinese competition in ag chemicals, and higher growth capex demands.
